DECLARATION OF ALEXANDER J. KOLT FILED BY DEFENDANT EXIGEN ELITE, INC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC., EXIGEN ELITE, INC AND EXIGEN MORTAGE ASSET MANAGEMENT, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473. THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON SEPTEMBER 16, 2014 IS VACATED. DEFENDANTS HAVE ESTABLISHED GROUNDS FOR RELIEF ARISING FROM THEIR MISTAKE AS TO MATERIAL FACTS RELATED TO THEIR DUTY TO RESPOND AND/OR MISCALENDARING. THERE WAS CONFUSION REGARDING THE DATE OF SERVICE. THERE WAS ALSO UNCERTAINTY ARISING FROM THE CHANGE IN THE NAME OF DEFENDANT EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC. TO EIS GROUP, INC. DEFENDANTS HAVE MOVED PROMPTLY FOR RELIEF AND CALIFORNIA HAS A STRONG POLICY OF HAVING CASES RESOLVED ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS WITHIN 15 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And Enter Default Of Defendant Exigen Mortgage Asset Management, Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order

PLAINTIFF IGOR KRAYCHIK's Motion To Strike And Enter Default Of Defendant Exigen Mortgage Asset Management, Incorporated is Granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendants To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DUSTIN L. COLLIER, TEL. NO. 767-0047 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PHILIP L. GREGORY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY LLP, TEL. NO. (650)697-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. MATTHEW H. LADNER OF THE PEPPER HAMILTON LLP, TEL, NO. (213)928-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT RETURN ON INTELLIGENCE BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC., EXIGEN ELITE, INC., EXIGEN MORTAGE ASSET MANAGEMENT, INC's Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action - Petition is granted as to Defendants Exigen Insurance Solutions, Inc. and Exigen Elite, Inc. Petition denied as moot as to Defendant Exigen Mortgage Asset Management, Inc. in light of the stipulation to withdraw the petition. A nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claims when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are "intimately founded in and intertwined" with the underlying contract obligations. (Boucher v. Alliance Title Company, Inc. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271.) By relying on contract terms in its claims against Defendants, Plaintiff is equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement. (Id. at 272.) Furthermore, Plaintiff has produced insufficient evidence to establish that the arbitration agreement was superseded and nullified. Additionally, the agreement is not unconscionable. The evidence presented establishes that the employment contract was negotiable as to job title, bonus, salary, moving allowance, and start date (Kraychik Decl., Ex. 1), and Plaintiff failed to present evidence that any other provision in the Offer Letter was not similarly negotiable. Furthermore, a failure to include or provide the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement alone is insufficient to support a finding of procedural unconscionability where the rules affect only Plaintiff's procedural rights. (Peng v. First Republic Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) Further, the arbitration agreement is not substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's argument as to lack of mutuality lacks merit because the agreement specifically limits its application to the employment relationship between Plaintiff and the signatory Defendant only, and the Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement alone does not compel a finding that the agreement lacks mutuality. Further, the qualifying language in the attorneys' fees provision keeps it from running afoul of Armendariz's minimum requirement that "the arbitration agreement ... cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 110-11.) Additionally, a mere possibility of the "repeat player effect"-without any evidence-is not enough to render an arbitration agreement unconscionable, and the agreement is not subject to unilateral modification or revocation. Defendants' request to stay the action pending completion of the arbitration proceedings is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sara Lerschen, CSR #6213 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DUSTIN L. COLLIER, TEL. NO. 767-0047 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PHILIP L. GREGORY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY, LLP, TEL. NO. (650)697-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. MEL M.C. COLE OF LITTLER MENDESON, P.C., TEL. NO. 276-2522 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EXIGEN EBIT;
ATTY. MATTHEW H. LADNER OF PEPPER HAMILTON LLP, TEL. NO. (213)928-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT RETURN ON INTELLIGENCE BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS EXIGEN HOLDINGS, INC., EXIGEN SERVICES, INC., EXIGEN, LTD., EXIGEN PROPERTIES, INC. AND EXIFAST'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action - Petition granted as to Defendants Exigen Holdings, Inc., Exigen Properties, Inc., and Exifast. Petition is denied as to Defendants Exigen Services, Inc. and Exigen, Ltd. in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's Motion to Strike. A nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claims when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are "intimately founded in and intertwined" with the underlying contract obligations. (Boucher v. Alliance Title Company, Inc. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271.) By relying on contract terms in its claims against Defendants, Plaintiff is equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement. (Id. at 272.) Furthermore, Plaintiff has produced insufficient evidence to establish that the arbitration agreement was superseded and nullified, nor is the agreement unconscionable. The evidence presented establishes that the employment contract was negotiable as to job title, bonus, salary, moving allowance, and start date ( Kraychik Decl., Ex. 1), and Plaintiff failed to present evidence that any other provision in the Offer Letter was not similarly negotiable. Furthermore, a failure to include or provide the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement alone is insufficient to support a finding of procedural unconscionability where the rules affect only Plaintiff's procedural rights. ((2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) Nor is the arbitration agreement substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's argument as to lack of mutuality lacks merit because the agreement specifically limits its application to the employment relationship between Plaintiff and the signatory Defendant only, and the Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement alone does not compel a finding that the agreement lacks mutuality. Further, the qualifying language in the attorneys' fees provision keeps it from running afoul of Armendariz's minimum requirement that "the arbitration agreement ... cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 110-11.) Additionally, the mere possibility of the "repeat player effect"-without any evidence-is not enough to render an arbitration agreement unconscionable, and the agreement is not subject to unilateral modification or revocation. Defendants' request to stay the action pending completion of the arbitration proceedings is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS EXIGEN INSURANCE SOLUTIONS, INC., EXIGEN ELITE, INC and EXIGEN MORTAGE ASSET MANAGEMENT, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is granted pursuant to CCP 473. The default entered on September 16, 2014 is vacated. Defendants have established grounds for relief arising from their mistake as to material facts related to their duty to respond and/or miscalendaring. There was confusion regarding the date of service. There was also uncertainty arising from the change in the name of defendant Exigen Insurance Solutions, Inc. to EIS Group, Inc. Defendants have moved promptly for relief and California has a strong policy of having cases resolved on the merits. Defendants shall file responsive pleadings within 15 days from entry of this order. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
